content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.

10	/-!
11	# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas
12	
13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
16	(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.
17	
18	The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
19	file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1: the closed-immersion universal property phrased as a
28	  topological range containment (under reduced source / target hypotheses).
29	
30	Substrate 2 (`gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain`, tensor-of-domains
31	over an algebraically-closed field via the polynomial / localisation iso
32	chain) is owed a later iter prover dispatch.
33	-/
34	
35	set_option autoImplicit false
36	set_option linter.style.setOption false
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology
41	
42	noncomputable section
43	
44	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
45	
46	namespace IsClosedImmersion
47	
48	/--
49	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
50	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
51	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
52	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
53	topological range of `i`.
54	
55	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
56	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
57	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
58	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
59	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
60	
61	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
62	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
63	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
64	-/
65	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
66	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
67	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
68	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
69	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
70	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
71	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
72	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
73	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
74	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
75	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
76	    intro hrange
77	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
78	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
79	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
80	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
81	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
82	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
83	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
84	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
85	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
86	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
87	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
88	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
89	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
90	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
91	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
92	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
93	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
94	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
95	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
96	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
97	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
98	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
99	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
100	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
101	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
102	      intro U
103	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
104	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
105	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
106	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
112	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
113	      calc i.ker
114	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
115	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
116	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
117	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
118	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
119	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
120	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
121	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
122	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
123	
124	end IsClosedImmersion
125	
126	end AlgebraicGeometry
127	
128	end

1	\chapter{Cross01 Substrate}
2	\label{chap:Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-189 plan-phase). This chapter is a project-bespoke
6	% substrate file landing two reusable lemmas needed to close the Lane B
7	% \(\mathbb{G}_m\)-scaling cocycle (path (III.c) of
8	% chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity) together with two off-target sorries
9	% (\texttt{gm\_geomIrred}, \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}) in
10	% \texttt{Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean}. Both substrates are
11	% generic in the geometric situation (the closed-immersion lift lemma is
12	% scheme-level; the tensor-domain lemma is ring-level keyed on the
13	% \texttt{projectiveLineBar} grading) and live in their own file rather
14	% than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling
15	% applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.
16	%
17	% The recipe consolidated below was produced by the
18	% \texttt{mathlib-analogist} subagent in iter-189 and is preserved
19	% verbatim in \texttt{analogies/lane-b-substrate.md} (Section
20	% ``Concrete project-side recipe''). The chapter itself is the
21	% prover-facing specification.
22	
23	\section{Setup and motivation}
24	\label{sec:cross01_substrate_setup}
25	
26	The Lane B cocycle \texttt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01} reduces, after
27	the iter-188 (III.c) separated-locus structural setup, to a single substantive
28	factorisation: a pair morphism
29	\(s_{\text{pair}} : T \to T_0 \times_X T_1\) into a fibre product over the
30	projective line bar \(\mathrm{PLB}_{\overline{k}}\) must factor through the
31	diagonal closed immersion
32	\(\Delta : T_0 \times_{X} T_1 \hookrightarrow T_0 \times_{\overline{k}} T_1\)
33	of the separated-over-\(\overline{k}\) target. Concretely the missing primitive is
34	the equivalence
35	\[
36	  \exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g \iff \mathrm{range}(g) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i)
37	\]
38	for a closed immersion \(i\) with reduced source/target hypothesis, which Mathlib
39	ships only in the kernel-inequality form (\(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\),
40	keyed on \(f.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)). Substrate~1 fills this gap.
41	
42	The same Lane B cocycle, together with the two off-target instances
43	\texttt{gm\_geomIrred} and \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}, also requires
44	\[
45	  \mathrm{IsDomain}\bigl(\mathrm{Away}_{\overline{k}}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\,\overline{k}\bigr)
46	\]
47	for a homogeneous generator \(f\) of positive degree in the
48	\(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading. Substrate~2 supplies this via a
49	canonical iso chain
50	\[
51	  \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \overline{k}[t]
52	  \;\cong\; \mathrm{MvPolynomial}_{\mathrm{Unit}}(\mathrm{Away}\,f)
53	\]
54	followed by localisation at \(X()\), reducing the claim to the
55	already-formalised pattern from \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}.
56	
57	\section{Closed-immersion lift by range inclusion}
58	\label{sec:lift_iff_range_subset}
59	
60	\begin{theorem}\leanok
61	[lift through closed immersion via range containment]
62	  \label{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}
63	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset}
64	  Let \(X,Y,Z\) be schemes (Type \(u\)), let \(i : Z \to X\) be a closed
65	  immersion with \(Z\) reduced, and let \(g : Y \to X\) be a
66	  quasi-compact morphism with \(Y\) reduced. Then there exists a
67	  morphism \(h : Y \to Z\) with \(h \circ i = g\) if and only if the
68	  topological range of \(g\) is contained in the topological range of
69	  \(i\):
70	  \[
71	    (\exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g)
72	    \;\iff\;
73	    \mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}).
74	  \]
75	\end{theorem}
76	\begin{proof}
77	
78	\leanok
79	  \emph{Forward direction} is range pushdown: if \(g = h \circ i\)
80	  then every point in \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}})\) is the image
81	  under \(i_{\text{base}}\) of \(h_{\text{base}}(y)\) for the
82	  corresponding preimage \(y\).
83	
84	  \emph{Backward direction} threads the Galois connection between
85	  vanishing-ideal and support of an ideal sheaf. The proof has six
86	  steps; the Mathlib primitives cited are present at the project's
87	  pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}).
88	
89	  \begin{enumerate}
90	  \item \emph{Range of a closed immersion is its kernel support.}
91	    Because \(i\) is a closed immersion, \(i\) factors (up to
92	    isomorphism) as the canonical subscheme inclusion of its kernel
93	    ideal sheaf, so
94	    \(\mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}) = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
95	    (Mathlib:
96	    \texttt{IdealSheafData.range\_subschemeι} +
97	    \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toImage}.)
98	  \item \emph{Range of \(g\) lies in \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).}
99	    The unconditional inclusion
100	    \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
101	    is shipped (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.range\_subset\_ker\_support}); under
102	    \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\), \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
103	    equals \(\overline{\mathrm{range}\,g_{\text{base}}}\)
104	    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.support\_ker}). Since
105	    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\) is closed and contains
106	    \(\mathrm{range}(g)\) by hypothesis, the closure also lies in
107	    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\):
108	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support} \subseteq i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
109	    (Mathlib: \texttt{closure\_minimal}.)
110	  \item \emph{\(Y\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(g.\mathrm{ker}\) is radical.}
111	    Under \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\),
112	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{ideal}\,U = \mathrm{ker}(g.\mathrm{app}\,U)\)
113	    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.ker\_apply}). Reducedness of \(Y\) makes this
114	    ring kernel a radical ideal pointwise on each affine open, so
115	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical} = g.\mathrm{ker}\).
116	  \item \emph{\(Z\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(i.\mathrm{ker}\) is
117	    radical.} Identical argument with \(i\) in place of \(g\)
118	    (closed immersions are quasi-compact, so the
119	    \texttt{ker\_apply} hypothesis is satisfied automatically).
120	  \item \emph{Galois-connection chain.} Combining Steps~2--4 with
121	    \texttt{IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal\_support} (which equates
122	    the vanishing ideal of a support with the radical) gives
123	    \[
124	      i.\mathrm{ker}
125	        = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
126	        = \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
127	        \le \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
128	        = g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
129	        = g.\mathrm{ker}.
130	    \]
131	    The middle inequality uses the antitone half of the Galois
132	    connection (\texttt{IdealSheafData.gc}).
133	  \item \emph{Apply Mathlib's kernel-inequality lift.} The proof
134	    concludes by feeding \(\mathrm{hker} : i.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)
135	    to \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift} and reading off its
136	    factorisation lemma \texttt{lift\_fac}.
137	  \end{enumerate}
138	\end{proof}
139	
140	\section{Tensor reducedness over the algebraic closure}
141	\label{sec:tensor_AwayGm_isDomain}
142	
143	\begin{theorem}[domain structure on \(\mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\)]
144	  \label{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
145	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
146	  Let \(\overline{k}\) be a field, let \(d \ge 1\), and let \(f\) be a
147	  non-zero homogeneous polynomial of degree \(d\) in the
148	  \(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading on
149	  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\). Then the
150	  tensor product
151	  \[
152	    \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\bigl(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\overline{k}\bigr)\,f
153	    \;\otimes_{\overline{k}}\;
154	    \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
155	  \]
156	  is a domain (and hence reduced).
157	\end{theorem}
158	\begin{proof}
159	  The proof exhibits a canonical isomorphism of \(\overline{k}\)-algebras
160	  identifying the tensor product with the localisation of a
161	  polynomial ring over a domain at a non-zero-divisor; both operations
162	  preserve the domain property.
163	
164	  \begin{enumerate}
165	  \item \emph{Domain instance on the homogeneous localisation.}
166	    \(\mathrm{Away}\,f := \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading})\,f\)
167	    embeds via the \texttt{val\_injective} bridge into
168	    \(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\,f\); since the polynomial ring
169	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\) is a domain
170	    and \(f \ne 0\), the localisation is a domain, and the bridge
171	    transports the property to \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\). (Already used in
172	    \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}; see
173	    \texttt{GmScaling.lean} L734--738 for the precise instance
174	    threading.)
175	  \item \emph{Collapse the tensor with the polynomial factor.}
176	    Recalling
177	    \(\mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
178	       = \mathrm{Localization.Away}(X()\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k})\),
179	    apply \texttt{MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv} to get
180	    \[
181	      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k}
182	      \;\cong\;
183	      \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f),
184	    \]
185	    a \(\overline{k}\)-algebra (in fact \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\)-algebra)
186	    isomorphism.
187	  \item \emph{Push the localisation through the tensor.}
188	    Tensoring with the localisation step
189	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k} \to \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}\)
190	    factors through Mathlib's tensor-base-change lemma
191	    \texttt{IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv}, yielding an
192	    isomorphism
193	    \[
194	      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
195	      \;\cong\;
196	      \mathrm{Localization.Away}(\iota(X())\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f))
197	    \]
198	    where \(\iota\) is the canonical algebra map. The threading uses
199	    \texttt{IsScalarTower} compatibility and the
200	    \texttt{Algebra}/\(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\) instance on the
201	    tensored polynomial ring.
202	  \item \emph{Right-hand side is a domain.}
203	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f)\) is a
204	    polynomial ring over a domain (Step~1), hence a domain by the
205	    standard instance. The generator \(X()\) is non-zero
206	    (\texttt{MvPolynomial.X\_ne\_zero}), so its powers form a
207	    submonoid of the non-zero-divisors
208	    (\texttt{powers\_le\_nonZeroDivisors\_of\_noZeroDivisors}); the
209	    localisation is then a domain by
210	    \texttt{IsLocalization.isDomain\_localization}.
211	  \end{enumerate}
212	
213	  Transferring the domain property along the iso of Step~3 closes the
214	  goal. Reducedness is a direct corollary of being a domain.
215	\end{proof}
216

1	# Analogy: Lane B substrate — `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` and tensor reducedness
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	lane-b-substrate
8	
9	## Iteration
10	189
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	For the Lane B (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`) iter-188 (III.c)
15	separated-locus structural setup, the substantive residual is a pair-
16	morphism factorization through the diagonal closed immersion. The
17	blueprint cites `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` as the
18	substrate; iter-188 verified this is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227. Two
19	questions:
20	
21	1. Is there a Mathlib variant of "morphism factors through closed
22	   immersion iff topological range subset" under any reasonable
23	   reduced-source hypothesis?
24	2. What is the cleanest project-side construction (with LOC), and what
25	   blocks the tensor-reducedness gap that also obstructs the other two
26	   off-target sorries (`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`)?
27	
28	## Project artifact(s)
29	
30	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:417-553` —
31	  `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` (the cocycle proof with the
32	  separated-locus structural setup landed iter-188; substantive sorry
33	  at line 553).
34	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:755-763` —
35	  `gm_geomIrred` (off-target sorry, same tensor gap).
36	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:779-795` —
37	  `projGm_isReduced` (off-target sorry, same tensor gap).
38	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:178-229` —
39	  `GmRing = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` and
40	  `gmRing_isDomain`.
41	- `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex:1505-1611` —
42	  (III.c) Separated-locus alternative recipe, including the iter-188
43	  `% NOTE` recording the substrate falsification.
44	
45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substantive substrate)
48	
49	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE at b80f227. The only `lift` variant shipped
50	  is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` with the kernel-inequality form
51	  (`f.ker ≤ g.ker`), at
52	  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:200-214`.
53	  The `lift_iff_range_subset` variant — equating factor-through with
54	  set-theoretic range containment under a reduced-source hypothesis —
55	  is not in Mathlib (verified iter-188 via `lean_leansearch` and
56	  re-verified iter-189 via `lean_leanfinder`).
57	
58	- **Mathlib substrate that DOES exist** (and which a project-side build
59	  would chain): the iter-189 search recovered the complete chain of
60	  primitives needed:
61	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`
62	    (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`):
63	    ideal-sheaf kernel of a scheme morphism.
64	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`
65	    (same file, `:698-734`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
66	    `f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom` on each affine open.
67	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.support_ker`
68	    (same file, `:839-878`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
69	    `f.ker.support = closure (Set.range f)`.
70	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.range_subset_ker_support`
71	    (same file, `:754-764`): the unconditional inclusion
72	    `Set.range f ⊆ f.ker.support`.
73	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`
74	    (same file, `:631-639`):
75	    `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical` — the Galois-connection
76	    pinning of "support determines radical".
77	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`
78	    (same file, `:622-625`):
79	    `GaloisConnection (support ·) (vanishingIdeal ·)` (with the
80	    `Closedsᵒᵈ` ordering).

108	### Decision 2: Tensor reducedness of `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)`
109	
110	This is the SECOND substrate needed, blocking three sorries
111	simultaneously (the cocycle factorization plus the two off-target
112	`gm_geomIrred` / `projGm_isReduced` instances at L755, L779).
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom check**: NONE direct. The closest shipped:
115	  - `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` class
116	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean:47`):
117	    requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗_k A)`. The bridge
118	    "k alg-closed ⟹ IsReduced ⟹ IsGeometricallyReduced k" is NOT
119	    shipped (the file's own TODO confirms: "for every field extension
120	    K of k the K-algebra K ⊗[k] A is reduced" — not landed for the
121	    general field-extension case).
122	  - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
123	    (same file, `:52`): an instance for `IsReduced (K ⊗_k A)` when
124	    `K/k` algebraic AND `A` geometrically reduced over `k`. Doesn't
125	    apply directly because neither factor here is algebraic over kbar.
126	  - `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective`
127	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean`): the abstract route
128	    "tensor of two domains embedding into a common domain with
129	    linearly-disjoint ranges is a domain". Building the LinearDisjoint
130	    witness for our concrete factors would be its own ~30-50 LOC
131	    sub-task.
132	
133	- **The clean project path** (the analogist's recommended route):
134	  exploit Mathlib's **localization–polynomial tensor product
135	  iso machinery**. The relevant Mathlib lemmas, both shipped:
136	  - `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
137	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`):
138	    `TensorProduct R A S ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away ((algebraMap R S) r)`
139	    when `A = Localization.Away r` over `R`. Tensor of a localization
140	    with anything is a localization (in the tensored ring).
141	  - `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`
142	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MvPolynomial.lean`):
143	    `TensorProduct R A (MvPolynomial σ R) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial σ A`.
144	    Tensor with a polynomial ring over the base is just polynomials
145	    over the algebra.
146	
147	  Both factors of our tensor are localizations of polynomial rings
148	  over kbar:
149	  - `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`
150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.
151	  - `Away (X_0·X_1) = HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projLineBarGrading) (X_0·X_1)`
152	    — a kbar-algebra that is a domain (already proven in
153	    `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` chain via the `val_injective`
154	    embedding into `Localization.Away (X_0·X_1)`).
155	
156	  Composition:
157	  1. `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar kbar[t] ≃ MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))`
158	     via `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`.
159	  2. The localization `kbar[t] → kbar[t][1/t] = GmRing` corresponds via
160	     `IsScalarTower` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv` to
161	     the localization at the same generator's image in the tensored
162	     polynomial ring.
163	  3. Result: `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing ≃ Localization.Away (X () in MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1)))`.
164	  4. `MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))` is a polynomial ring over a
165	     domain, hence a domain.
166	  5. Localization at powers of `X ()` (a non-zero divisor in the
167	     polynomial ring over a domain) preserves the domain property
168	     via `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` +
169	     `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors`
170	     + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` (already in the project's playbook,
171	     used in `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` L734-738).
172	
173	  Therefore `IsDomain (Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing)` follows, and
174	  `IsReduced` follows from `IsDomain` directly.
175	
176	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — buildable at ~50-80 LOC.
177	  No new Mathlib infrastructure needed; everything is a chain of
178	  existing iso lemmas. The same pattern unifies the `gm_geomIrred` /
179	  `projGm_isReduced` closures (same generators, different chart degrees).

255	## Concrete project-side recipe
256	
257	### Substrate 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
258	
259	Target signature:
260	
261	```lean
262	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset
263	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
264	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
265	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔
266	      Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base
267	```
268	
269	Body skeleton (with `≈ 40-60` LOC):
270	
271	```lean
272	  constructor
273	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: trivial range pushdown.
274	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
275	    intro x ⟨y, rfl⟩
276	    exact ⟨h.base y, rfl⟩
277	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substance.
278	    intro hrange
279	    -- Step 1: closed immersion ⟹ range is closed, equal to ker.support.
280	    have hi_range : Set.range i.base = (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
281	      rw [← IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι]
282	      -- Use that i = subschemeι i.ker (up to iso) via toImage_imageι
283	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC bridge using Scheme.Hom.toImage / imageι
284	    -- Step 2: g.ker.support = closure (range g) ⊆ i.ker.support
285	    --         (latter is closed).
286	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := by
287	      rw [Hom.support_ker]
288	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.isClosed_supportSet
289	      exact hrange.trans hi_range.le
290	    -- Step 3: Y reduced ⟹ g.ker is radical
291	    --         (since g.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (g.app U)
292	    --          and Y reduced ⟹ that kernel is radical).
293	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
294	      ext U : 2
295	      simp only [IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, g.ker_apply]
296	      exact (RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective
297	        Function.Surjective.rfl).mpr inferInstance  -- needs adjustment
298	      -- Concretely: use `f.app U`'s kernel-is-radical because Y is reduced;
299	      -- the appropriate lemma is `RingHom.ker_isRadical_of_reduced_codomain`.
300	      sorry  -- ~10 LOC; standard "reduced source ⟹ kernel of ring map radical"
301	    -- Step 4: Z reduced ⟹ i.ker is radical (same argument).
302	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
303	      ext U : 2
304	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC, symmetric to Step 3
305	    -- Step 5: combine via Galois connection.
306	    --         g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support
307	    --         ⟹ vanishingIdeal(i.ker.support) ≤ vanishingIdeal(g.ker.support)
308	    --         ⟹ i.ker.radical ≤ g.ker.radical (by `vanishingIdeal_support`)
309	    --         ⟹ i.ker ≤ g.ker (by Steps 3, 4).
310	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
311	      calc i.ker
312	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
313	        _ = vanishingIdeal i.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_support.symm
314	        _ ≤ vanishingIdeal g.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
315	        _ = g.ker.radical := vanishingIdeal_support
316	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
317	    -- Step 6: apply Mathlib's IsClosedImmersion.lift.
318	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker,
319	           IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
320	```
321	
322	**Notes on the LOC**:
323	- The two "kernel-is-radical" steps (3, 4) might need a Mathlib bridge
324	  lemma not directly searched here; if so, that adds ~10-15 LOC for an
325	  inline derivation (use `Hom.ker_apply` to drop to ring kernels, then
326	  reducedness of the source gives radicality of the ring kernel — a
327	  standard `RingHom.ker_isRadical` argument).
328	- The Step 1 bridge (closed immersion `i` is the `subschemeι` of its
329	  kernel, up to iso) uses `Scheme.Hom.toImage_imageι` from Mathlib.
330	
331	**Total**: ~40-60 LOC.

333	### Substrate 2: tensor reducedness `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar GmRing`
334	
335	Target signature (generic in the homogeneous generator `f` of degree `d`):
336	
337	```lean
338	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain
339	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {d : ℕ} (hd : 0 < d)
340	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
341	    (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar d)
342	    (hf_ne : f ≠ 0) :
343	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
344	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f)
345	      (GmRing kbar))
346	```
347	
348	Body skeleton (with `≈ 50-80` LOC):
349	
350	```lean
351	  -- Step 1: Away 𝒜 f is a domain (project-side, used in
352	  -- projectiveLineBar_isReduced L734-738).
353	  haveI hf_dom : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
354	      (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) := by
355	    refine Function.Injective.isDomain
356	      (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ f) (Localization.Away f)) ?_
357	    · intro x y h
358	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
359	  -- Step 2: Build the iso chain.
360	  -- (a) MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv :
361	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃ₐ[Away 𝒜 f]
362	  --       MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f)
363	  -- (b) IsScalarTower over kbar lifts the localization at X () in MvPoly Unit kbar
364	  --     to the corresponding localization at X () in MvPoly Unit (Away 𝒜 f).
365	  -- (c) Resulting iso:
366	  --     TensorProduct kbar (Away 𝒜 f) (GmRing kbar) ≃
367	  --       Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f))
368	  -- Step 3: RHS is a domain.
369	  -- MvPolynomial Unit (Away 𝒜 f) is a polynomial ring over a domain,
370	  -- hence a domain (instance auto-resolves).
371	  -- Localization at powers of X () (a non-zero divisor, by
372	  -- MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero) preserves the domain property via
373	  -- IsLocalization.isDomain_localization + powers_le_nonZeroDivisors.
374	  sorry  -- ~50-80 LOC threading the iso chain and applying domain instances
375	```

379	localization step, after collapsing the `Away 𝒜 f ⊗_kbar kbar[t]` tensor

382	the `Algebra` from polynomial ring over an algebra, the `IsLocalization.Away`

389	```

177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance

12	
13	/-!
14	# Localized Module
15	
16	Given a commutative semiring `R`, a multiplicative subset `S ⊆ R` and an `R`-module `M`, we can
17	localize `M` by `S`. This gives us a `Localization S`-module.
18	
19	## Main definition
20	
21	* `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` : A localization of modules corresponds to a base change.
22	-/
23	
24	@[expose] public section
25	
26	variable {R : Type*} [CommSemiring R] (S : Submonoid R)
27	  (A : Type*) [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization S A]
28	  {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
29	  {M' : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M'] [Module R M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower R A M']
30	  (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
31	
32	/-- The forward direction of `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange`. It is also used to prove the
33	other direction. -/
34	theorem IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange [IsLocalizedModule S f] : IsBaseChange A f :=
35	  .of_lift_unique _ fun Q _ _ _ _ g ↦ by
36	    obtain ⟨ℓ, rfl, h₂⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.is_universal S f g fun s ↦ by
37	      rw [← (Algebra.lsmul R (A := A) R Q).commutes]; exact (IsLocalization.map_units A s).map _
38	    refine ⟨ℓ.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization S A, by simp, fun g'' h ↦ ?_⟩
39	    cases h₂ (LinearMap.restrictScalars R g'') h; rfl
40	
41	/-- The map `(f : M →ₗ[R] M')` is a localization of modules iff the map
42	`(Localization S) × M → N, (s, m) ↦ s • f m` is the tensor product (insomuch as it is the universal
43	bilinear map).
44	In particular, there is an isomorphism between `LocalizedModule S M` and `(Localization S) ⊗[R] M`
45	given by `m/s ↦ (1/s) ⊗ₜ m`.
46	-/
47	theorem isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange : IsLocalizedModule S f ↔ IsBaseChange A f := by
48	  refine ⟨fun _ ↦ IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange S A f, fun h ↦ ?_⟩
49	  letI : Module A (LocalizedModule S M) := LocalizedModule.moduleOfIsLocalization ..
50	  have : IsBaseChange A (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M) := IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange S A _
51	  let e := (this.equiv.symm.trans h.equiv).restrictScalars R
52	  convert IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv S (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M) e
53	  ext
54	  rw [LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply,
55	    LinearEquiv.restrictScalars_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply, IsBaseChange.equiv_symm_apply,
56	    IsBaseChange.equiv_tmul, one_smul]
57	
58	open TensorProduct
59	
60	variable (M) in

260	      IsLocalization.mk' (A ⊗[R] S) (algebraMap R S x * s)
261	        ⟨algebraMap R S y.1, Algebra.mem_algebraMapSubmonoid_of_mem _⟩ := by
262	  simp [IsLocalization.eq_mk'_iff_mul_eq, map_mul,
263	    RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
264	
265	namespace Localization
266	
267	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (M : Submonoid R) (Rₘ : Type*) [CommRing Rₘ] [Algebra R Rₘ]
268	  (S : Type*) [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
269	
270	/-- The isomorphism `S ⊗[R] Rₘ ≃ₐ[S] Sₘ`. This is a specialization of `IsLocalization.algEquiv`,
271	but with additional properties since now `Sₘ` is automatically an `Rₘ`-algebra. -/
272	noncomputable def tensorLeftAlgEquiv :
273	    (S ⊗[R] Localization M) ≃ₐ[S] Localization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S M) :=
274	  (algEquiv (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S M) (S ⊗[R] Localization M)).symm
275	
276	variable {S} in
277	@[simp]
278	theorem tensorLeftAlgEquiv_apply_tmul_one (x : S) :
279	    tensorLeftAlgEquiv M S (x ⊗ₜ[R] 1) = algebraMap _ _ x :=
280	  (tensorLeftAlgEquiv M S).commutes x
281	
282	@[simp]
283	theorem tensorLeftAlgEquiv_apply_one_tmul (x : Localization M) :
284	    tensorLeftAlgEquiv M S (1 ⊗ₜ[R] x) = algebraMap _ _ x := by
285	  let Rₘ := Localization M
286	  let Sₘ := Localization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S M)
287	  obtain ⟨x, y, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.exists_mk'_eq M x
288	  letI : Algebra Rₘ (S ⊗[R] Rₘ) := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
289	  have h1 : (1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] IsLocalization.mk' Rₘ x y = algebraMap _ _ (IsLocalization.mk' Rₘ x y) :=
290	    rfl
291	  rw [h1, tensorLeftAlgEquiv, algEquiv_symm_apply,
292	    IsLocalization.algebraMap_mk' S, IsLocalization.map_mk', IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul]
293	  simp_rw [RingHom.id_apply]
294	  have h x : algebraMap S Sₘ ((algebraMap R S) x) = algebraMap Rₘ Sₘ ((algebraMap R Rₘ) x) := by
295	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply]
296	  rw [h, h, ← map_mul, IsLocalization.mk'_spec]
297	
298	/-- The isomorphism `Rₘ ⊗[R] S ≃ₐ[Rₘ] Sₘ`. -/
299	noncomputable def tensorRightAlgEquiv :
300	    Localization M ⊗[R] S ≃ₐ[Localization M] Localization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid S M) where
301	  __ := (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm R (Localization M) S).toRingEquiv.trans
302	    (tensorLeftAlgEquiv M S).toRingEquiv
303	  commutes' := tensorLeftAlgEquiv_apply_one_tmul M S
304	
305	@[simp]
306	theorem tensorRightAlgEquiv_apply_tmul_one (x : Localization M) :
307	    tensorRightAlgEquiv M S (x ⊗ₜ[R] 1) = algebraMap _ _ x :=
308	  (tensorRightAlgEquiv M S).commutes x
309	
310	variable {S} in
311	@[simp]
312	theorem tensorRightAlgEquiv_apply_one_tmul (x : S) :
313	    tensorRightAlgEquiv M S (1 ⊗ₜ[R] x) = algebraMap _ _ x :=
314	  (tensorLeftAlgEquiv M S).commutes x
315	
316	end Localization
317	
318	variable (R S) {A} in
319	/-- `A[M⁻¹] ⊗[R] S` is the localization of `A ⊗[R] S` at `M`. -/
320	lemma IsLocalization.tensorProduct_tensorProduct (M : Submonoid A)
321	    (B : Type*) [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [Algebra A B] [IsScalarTower R A B]
322	    [IsLocalization M B]
323	    [Algebra (A ⊗[R] S) (B ⊗[R] S)] [IsScalarTower A (A ⊗[R] S) (B ⊗[R] S)]
324	    (H : (algebraMap (A ⊗[R] S) (B ⊗[R] S)).comp Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight.toRingHom =
325	      Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight.toRingHom) :
326	    IsLocalization (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid (A ⊗[R] S) M) (B ⊗[R] S) :=
327	  (Algebra.isLocalization_iff_isPushout M _).mpr
328	    (Algebra.IsPushout.tensorProduct_tensorProduct R S A B H).symm
329	
330	namespace IsLocalization.Away
331	
332	instance tensor [IsLocalization.Away r A] :
333	    IsLocalization.Away (algebraMap R S r) (S ⊗[R] A) := by
334	  simp only [IsLocalization.Away, ← Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid_powers]
335	  infer_instance
336	
337	variable (S) in
338	/-- The `S`-isomorphism `S ⊗[R] Rᵣ ≃ₐ Sᵣ`. -/
339	noncomputable abbrev tensorEquiv [IsLocalization.Away r A] :
340	    S ⊗[R] A ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away (algebraMap R S r) :=
341	  IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers <| algebraMap R S r) _ _
342	
343	attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
344	
345	instance tensorRight [IsLocalization.Away r A] :
346	    IsLocalization.Away (algebraMap R S r) (A ⊗[R] S) := by
347	  simp only [IsLocalization.Away, ← Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid_powers]
348	  infer_instance

144	/-- Given `x : R`, a localization map `F : R →+* S` away from `x`, and a map of `CommSemiring`s
145	`g : R →+* P` such that `g x` is invertible, the homomorphism induced from `S` to `P` sending
146	`z : S` to `g y * (g x)⁻ⁿ`, where `y : R, n : ℕ` are such that `z = F y * (F x)⁻ⁿ`. -/
147	noncomputable def lift (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : S →+* P :=
148	  IsLocalization.lift fun y : Submonoid.powers x =>
149	    show IsUnit (g y.1) by
150	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := y.2
151	      rw [← hn, g.map_pow]
152	      exact IsUnit.map (powMonoidHom n : P →* P) hg

182	/-- Given a map `f : R →+* S` and an element `r : R`, we may construct a map `Rᵣ →+* Sᵣ`. -/
183	noncomputable def map (f : R →+* P) (r : R) [IsLocalization.Away r S]
184	    [IsLocalization.Away (f r) Q] : S →+* Q :=
185	  IsLocalization.map Q f
186	    (show Submonoid.powers r ≤ (Submonoid.powers (f r)).comap f by
187	      rintro x ⟨n, rfl⟩
188	      use n
189	      simp)

191	lemma map_injective_iff {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
192	    (f : R →+* P) (r : R) [IsLocalization.Away r S]
193	    [IsLocalization.Away (f r) Q] :
194	    Function.Injective (map S Q f r) ↔ ∀ a, f a = 0 → ∃ n, r ^ n * a = 0 := by
195	  rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
196	  trans ∀ a n, f r ^ n * f a = 0 → ∃ m, r ^ m * a = 0
197	  · simp [(IsLocalization.mk'_surjective (.powers r)).forall, Submonoid.mem_powers_iff,
198	      IsLocalization.Away.map, IsLocalization.map_mk', IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff]
199	  · refine ⟨fun H x hx ↦ H x 0 (by simpa), fun H a n ha ↦ ?_⟩
200	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ := H (r ^ n * a) (by simpa)
201	    exact ⟨m + n, by simp [pow_add, mul_assoc, *]⟩
202	
203	lemma map_surjective_iff (f : R →+* P) (r : R) [IsLocalization.Away r S]
204	    [IsLocalization.Away (f r) Q] :
205	    Function.Surjective (map S Q f r) ↔ ∀ a, ∃ b m, f b = f r ^ m * a := by
206	  trans ∀ a n, ∃ b m k, f (r ^ (k + n) * b) = f r ^ (k + m) * a
207	  · simp [Function.Surjective, (IsLocalization.mk'_surjective (.powers (f r))).forall, ← map_pow,
208	      (IsLocalization.mk'_surjective (.powers r)).exists, Submonoid.mem_powers_iff, pow_add,
209	      IsLocalization.Away.map, IsLocalization.map_mk', ← mul_assoc,
210	      IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq, ← map_mul, IsLocalization.eq_iff_exists (.powers (f r))]
211	  · refine ⟨fun H x ↦ ⟨_, _, (H x 0).choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec⟩, fun H a n ↦ ?_⟩
212	    obtain ⟨b, m, e⟩ := H a
213	    exact ⟨b, n + m, 0, by simp [e, pow_add]; ring_nf⟩

57	/-- Given `x : R` and a localization map `F : R →+* S` away from `x`, `invSelf` is `(F x)⁻¹`. -/
58	noncomputable def invSelf : S :=
59	  mk' S (1 : R) ⟨x, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
60

62	theorem mul_invSelf : algebraMap R S x * invSelf x = 1 := by
63	  convert IsLocalization.mk'_mul_mk'_eq_one (M := Submonoid.powers x) (S := S) _ 1
64	  symm
65	  apply IsLocalization.mk'_one
66	
67	/-- For `s : S` with `S` being the localization of `R` away from `x`,
68	this is a choice of `(r, n) : R × ℕ` such that `s * algebraMap R S (x ^ n) = algebraMap R S r`. -/
69	noncomputable def sec (s : S) : R × ℕ :=
70	  ⟨(IsLocalization.sec (Submonoid.powers x) s).1,
71	   (IsLocalization.sec (Submonoid.powers x) s).2.property.choose⟩

75	  simp only [IsLocalization.Away.sec, ← IsLocalization.sec_spec]
76	  congr

79	lemma algebraMap_pow_isUnit (n : ℕ) : IsUnit (algebraMap R S x ^ n) :=

81

88	  exact associated_mul_unit_left _ _ <| .pow _ <| IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _

90	lemma algebraMap_isUnit_iff {y : R} : IsUnit (algebraMap R S y) ↔ ∃ n, y ∣ x ^ n :=
91	  (IsLocalization.algebraMap_isUnit_iff <| .powers x).trans <| by simp [Submonoid.mem_powers_iff]
92	
93	lemma surj (z : S) : ∃ (n : ℕ) (a : R), z * algebraMap R S x ^ n = algebraMap R S a := by
94	  obtain ⟨⟨a, ⟨-, n, rfl⟩⟩, h⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers x) z
95	  use n, a
96	  simpa using h
97	
98	lemma exists_of_eq {a b : R} (h : algebraMap R S a = algebraMap R S b) :
99	    ∃ (n : ℕ), x ^ n * a = x ^ n * b := by
100	  obtain ⟨⟨-, n, rfl⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalization.exists_of_eq (M := Submonoid.powers x) h
101	  use n
102	
103	/-- Specialized constructor for `IsLocalization.Away`. -/
104	lemma mk (r : R) (map_unit : IsUnit (algebraMap R S r))
105	    (surj : ∀ s, ∃ (n : ℕ) (a : R), s * algebraMap R S r ^ n = algebraMap R S a)
106	    (exists_of_eq : ∀ a b, algebraMap R S a = algebraMap R S b → ∃ (n : ℕ), r ^ n * a = r ^ n * b) :
107	    IsLocalization.Away r S where
108	  map_units := by
109	    rintro ⟨-, n, rfl⟩
110	    simp only [map_pow]
111	    exact IsUnit.pow _ map_unit
112	  surj z := by
113	    obtain ⟨n, a, hn⟩ := surj z
114	    use ⟨a, ⟨r ^ n, n, rfl⟩⟩
115	    simpa using hn
116	  exists_of_eq {x y} h := by
117	    obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := exists_of_eq x y h
118	    use ⟨r ^ n, n, rfl⟩
119	
120	lemma of_associated {r r' : R} (h : Associated r r') [IsLocalization.Away r S] :
121	    IsLocalization.Away r' S := by
122	  obtain ⟨u, rfl⟩ := h
123	  refine mk _ ?_ (fun s ↦ ?_) (fun a b hab ↦ ?_)
124	  · simp [algebraMap_isUnit r, IsUnit.map _ u.isUnit]
125	  · obtain ⟨n, a, hn⟩ := surj r s
126	    use n, a * u ^ n
127	    simp [mul_pow, ← mul_assoc, hn]
128	  · obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := exists_of_eq r hab
129	    use n
130	    rw [mul_pow, mul_comm (r ^ n), mul_assoc, mul_assoc, hn]
131	
132	/-- If `r` and `r'` are associated elements of `R`, an `R`-algebra `S`
133	is the localization of `R` away from `r` if and only if it is the localization of `R` away from
134	`r'`. -/
135	lemma iff_of_associated {r r' : R} (h : Associated r r') :
136	    IsLocalization.Away r S ↔ IsLocalization.Away r' S :=
137	  ⟨fun _ ↦ IsLocalization.Away.of_associated h, fun _ ↦ IsLocalization.Away.of_associated h.symm⟩
138	
139	lemma isUnit_of_dvd {r : R} (h : r ∣ x) : IsUnit (algebraMap R S r) :=
140	  isUnit_of_dvd_unit (map_dvd _ h) (algebraMap_isUnit x)
141	
142	variable {g : R →+* P}

144	/-- Given `x : R`, a localization map `F : R →+* S` away from `x`, and a map of `CommSemiring`s

146	`z : S` to `g y * (g x)⁻ⁿ`, where `y : R, n : ℕ` are such that `z = F y * (F x)⁻ⁿ`. -/
147	noncomputable def lift (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : S →+* P :=
148	  IsLocalization.lift fun y : Submonoid.powers x =>
149	    show IsUnit (g y.1) by
150	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := y.2
151	      rw [← hn, g.map_pow]
152	      exact IsUnit.map (powMonoidHom n : P →* P) hg

155	theorem lift_eq (hg : IsUnit (g x)) (a : R) : lift x hg (algebraMap R S a) = g a :=
156	  IsLocalization.lift_eq _ _
157

159	theorem lift_comp (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : (lift x hg).comp (algebraMap R S) = g :=
160	  IsLocalization.lift_comp _

163	respectively, the homomorphism induced from `S` to `P`. -/
164	noncomputable def awayToAwayLeft (y : R) [Algebra R P] [IsLocalization.Away (y * x) P] : S →+* P :=
165	  lift x <| isUnit_of_dvd (y * x) (dvd_mul_left _ _)

155	def lift (f : A →ₐ[S] C) (g : B →ₐ[R] C) (hfg : ∀ x y, Commute (f x) (g y)) : (A ⊗[R] B) →ₐ[S] C :=
156	  algHomOfLinearMapTensorProduct
157	    (AlgebraTensorModule.lift <|
158	      letI restr : (C →ₗ[S] C) →ₗ[S] _ :=
159	        { toFun := (·.restrictScalars R)
160	          map_add' := fun _ _ => LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
161	          map_smul' := fun _ _ => LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl }
162	      LinearMap.flip <| (restr ∘ₗ LinearMap.mul S C ∘ₗ f.toLinearMap).flip ∘ₗ g)
163	    (fun a₁ a₂ b₁ b₂ => show f (a₁ * a₂) * g (b₁ * b₂) = f a₁ * g b₁ * (f a₂ * g b₂) by
164	      rw [map_mul, map_mul, (hfg a₂ b₁).mul_mul_mul_comm])

181	    (lift f g hfg).comp includeLeft = f :=

195	/-- The universal property of the tensor product of algebras.
196	
197	Pairs of algebra morphisms that commute are equivalent to algebra morphisms from the tensor product.
198	
199	This is `Algebra.TensorProduct.lift` as an equivalence.
200	
201	See also `GradedTensorProduct.liftEquiv` for an alternative commutativity requirement for graded
202	algebra. -/
203	@[simps]
204	def liftEquiv : {fg : (A →ₐ[S] C) × (B →ₐ[R] C) // ∀ x y, Commute (fg.1 x) (fg.2 y)}
205	    ≃ ((A ⊗[R] B) →ₐ[S] C) where
206	  toFun fg := lift fg.val.1 fg.val.2 fg.prop
207	  invFun f' := ⟨(f'.comp includeLeft, (f'.restrictScalars R).comp includeRight), fun _ _ =>
208	    ((Commute.one_right _).tmul (Commute.one_left _)).map f'⟩
209	  left_inv fg := by ext <;> simp
210	  right_inv f' := by ext <;> simp
211	
212	end lift

46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar

50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :

53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/

64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))

123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity

import Mathlib

/-!
# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover

This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:

* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
